figures.caption: Figure 1: LLM agents face a fundamental device–cloud dilemma. To address this, Hera is proposed as a lightweight step-level device–cloud coordinator trained via a two-stage paradigm: imitation learning for cold-start, followed by reinforcement learning for performance–cost co-optimization.
Table 1: Task success rate, cloud LLM API cost, and end-to-end trajectory latency on ALFWorld, WebShop, and AppWorld, averaged over three runs.
Figure 2: Step-level performance comparison of device Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and cloud Qwen-Max on ALFWorld and WebShop tasks.
Table 3: Task success rate, cloud LLM API cost, and end-to-end trajectory latency on ALFWorld across different device–cloud model pairs, averaged over three runs.
